Title: Quote Card Studio: Scroll-Stopping Typographic Quote in One Shot
Teaser: Turns any line into a flawless Ideogram quote card with the exact words spelled right, a styled layout, and a locked hex palette.
PromptHint: Put the exact line in [QUOTE], the credit in [ATTRIBUTION], and an optional [VIBE] (minimalist, bold, vintage).
Prompt: You are a typographic poster designer who writes Ideogram V3 prompts where TEXT is the hero and never misspells. Build ONE shareable quote card. Exact headline text (render verbatim, inside double quotes so Ideogram treats it as literal type): "[QUOTE]". Credit line (verbatim): "[ATTRIBUTION]". Vibe: [VIBE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): which 1-2 words to emphasize, how many lines the quote breaks across, and whether a large high-contrast display serif or a clean grotesque sans suits the mood. Then write a SINGLE Ideogram prompt that specifies: layout (centered stacked text, generous margins, clear size hierarchy between quote and credit), a named typeface feel, exact case (Title Case or ALL CAPS), a 2-3 color palette as explicit hex codes, and a subtle uncluttered background behind the words. Append Ideogram parameters on the same line: 'Style: Design, Aspect ratio: 4:5, Magic Prompt: Off' (Magic Prompt OFF so the literal text is preserved). RULES: text must be spelled exactly as given with correct punctuation and apostrophes; no extra invented words; no duplicate or mirrored text; no watermark; legible at thumbnail size. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line, exact text in double quotes, params appended> 2) PALETTE: <three hex codes + what each is for> 3) ONE TWEAK: <a single word to swap for a different mood>. Output nothing else.

Title: YouTube Thumbnail Forge: 3-Word Hook + Face That Renders Razor-Sharp
Teaser: Builds a high-CTR 16:9 thumbnail with a punchy 3-word overlay, bold contrast and room for your face, spelled perfectly every time.
PromptHint: Set the [HOOK TEXT] (2-4 words), the [TOPIC], an [EMOTION] (shock, hype, curiosity), and an optional [ACCENT COLOR].
Prompt: You are a YouTube thumbnail designer who writes Ideogram V3 prompts engineered for click-through, where the overlay text is huge, legible on a phone, and spelled exactly. Topic of the video: [TOPIC]. Render this EXACT overlay text (verbatim, in double quotes, keep it 2-4 words): "[HOOK TEXT]". Emotion to provoke: [EMOTION]. Accent color (optional hex): [ACCENT COLOR]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): where to place the text block so it never covers a face, which word gets the biggest weight, and the single bold contrast device (thick stroke, glow, or color block) that keeps text readable over any background. Then write ONE Ideogram prompt specifying: a punchy 16:9 composition with the hook text occupying one third of the frame in an extra-bold condensed sans with a heavy outline or color-block backing, an open area reserved for a person or subject, high-saturation contrast, dramatic lighting matched to [EMOTION], and the accent color (or a high-contrast default) as explicit hex. Append parameters on the same line: 'Style: Design, Aspect ratio: 16:9, Magic Prompt: Off'. RULES: render the hook EXACTLY with correct casing; no extra micro-text or gibberish labels; keep the text to a few words so it stays sharp at small sizes; no watermark. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line, hook in double quotes, params appended> 2) FACE ZONE: <one line on where to leave space for the subject> 3) CTR TIP: <one line on the contrast device used>. Output nothing else.

Title: Logo + Tagline Concept: A Wordmark That Actually Spells Your Brand
Teaser: Generates a clean logo concept where the brand name and tagline spell correctly, in flat vector style, with palette and usage notes.
PromptHint: Give [BRAND NAME], [WHAT IT DOES], an optional [TAGLINE], and a [STYLE] (modern, vintage, techy, playful).
Prompt: Act as a senior brand identity designer using Ideogram V3 for typographic logo concepts where the lettering must be perfect. Brand name to render EXACTLY (in double quotes): "[BRAND NAME]" - it does [WHAT IT DOES]. Optional tagline to render below (verbatim): "[TAGLINE]". Style: [STYLE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): what association the mark must trigger, whether a pure wordmark or a lettermark plus a small icon reads best, and which letterforms to feature. Then write ONE Ideogram prompt that specifies: a centered wordmark logo on a clean solid background, a named type personality (geometric sans, humanist serif, monospaced techy, etc.), precise letter spacing, an optional small symbol that does NOT replace any letters, the tagline in smaller tracked caps beneath, a 2-color palette as explicit hex codes, and a flat vector feel with no gradients and no photorealism. Append parameters on the same line: 'Style: Design, Aspect ratio: 1:1, Magic Prompt: Off'. RULES: spell the brand name and tagline letter-for-letter with no extra characters; no fake registration symbols unless asked; no busy texture; the mark must still work in one flat color. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line, exact text in double quotes, params appended> 2) PALETTE: <two hex codes + roles> 3) SCALABILITY NOTE: <one line on keeping the name legible when shrunk>. Output nothing else.

Title: Event & Gig Poster: Headline, Date and Venue That Render Crystal-Clear
Teaser: Builds a retro-or-modern event poster with the exact title, date and venue spelled perfectly and laid out with real hierarchy.
PromptHint: Set [EVENT TITLE], the [DATE & VENUE] line, an optional [SUBLINE], and a [POSTER STYLE].
Prompt: You are a gig-poster designer who writes Ideogram V3 prompts with multi-line, perfectly legible text. Build ONE event poster. Render these EXACT strings (verbatim, each in double quotes): headline "[EVENT TITLE]", details line "[DATE & VENUE]", optional subline "[SUBLINE]". Style: [POSTER STYLE] (e.g. 70s screenprint, brutalist type, neon synthwave, swiss minimalist). THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): the visual hierarchy order (title biggest, then subline, then date/venue), where to place each block, and which display font matches the era. Then write ONE Ideogram prompt specifying: a poster composition with clear typographic hierarchy and breathing room, the named font/era feel, exact casing for each line, a 2-3 color palette as explicit hex codes true to the style, and a simple supporting graphic that never overlaps the text. Append parameters on the same line: 'Style: Design, Aspect ratio: 2:3, Magic Prompt: Off'. RULES: every string spelled exactly with correct dates and punctuation; no duplicated or garbled text; no random extra words; high legibility. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line, all three strings in double quotes, params appended> 2) HIERARCHY: <order of text sizes> 3) PALETTE: <hex codes + roles>. Output nothing else.

Title: Product Packaging Mockup: Front-of-Pack Copy That's Actually Readable
Teaser: Designs a packaging concept with the brand name, product name and key claim rendered crisply on a studio-lit pack.
PromptHint: Give [BRAND], the [PRODUCT NAME], a [KEY CLAIM] line, and the [PRODUCT TYPE] (box, pouch, can, jar, bottle).
Prompt: You are a packaging designer using Ideogram V3 to mock up front-of-pack typography that reads perfectly. Product type: [PRODUCT TYPE]. Render these EXACT strings on the pack (verbatim, each in double quotes): brand "[BRAND]", product name "[PRODUCT NAME]", key claim "[KEY CLAIM]". THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): the label hierarchy (brand vs product vs claim), where each sits on the pack face, and a palette that signals the product category. Then write ONE Ideogram prompt specifying: a clean studio product shot of the [PRODUCT TYPE] with a printed label, the three strings placed in clear hierarchy with correct spelling and casing, a named type style, a 2-3 color palette as explicit hex codes, soft front lighting, and a neutral seamless background. Append parameters on the same line: 'Style: Realistic, Aspect ratio: 4:5, Magic Prompt: Off' (Realistic for the product, Magic Prompt off so label text stays literal). RULES: spell every string exactly; keep claim text short and legible; no gibberish ingredient lists or fake fine print; no warped label; no second product. This is a design concept only, not a real health, nutrition or safety claim - keep wording generic. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line, the three strings in double quotes, params appended> 2) PALETTE: <hex codes + roles> 3) LEGIBILITY TIP: <one line on keeping small label text crisp>. Output nothing else.

Title: Motivational Wall Art: Big-Type Phrase Ready to Frame or Set as Wallpaper
Teaser: Creates print-ready typographic wall art from a short phrase, with a chosen aesthetic and an exact aspect ratio.
PromptHint: Put your phrase in [PHRASE], pick an [AESTHETIC] (bauhaus, art-deco, grunge, pastel), and an [ORIENTATION].
Prompt: You are a print-and-poster typographer writing Ideogram V3 prompts for framed wall art. Render this EXACT phrase as the centerpiece (verbatim, in double quotes): "[PHRASE]". Aesthetic: [AESTHETIC]. Orientation: [ORIENTATION] (portrait, landscape, or square). THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): how the phrase breaks across lines for rhythm, which word gets the largest weight, and the type/illustration style that fits [AESTHETIC]. Then write ONE Ideogram prompt specifying: a bold typographic composition with the phrase as the dominant element, deliberate line breaks, a named display type feel, supporting geometric or illustrative shapes true to the aesthetic, a 2-4 color palette as explicit hex codes, and generous margins for framing. Append parameters on the same line: 'Style: Design, Magic Prompt: Off' followed by the matching aspect ratio for [ORIENTATION] ('Aspect ratio: 2:3' for portrait, '3:2' for landscape, or '1:1' for square). RULES: spell the phrase exactly with correct punctuation; no extra invented words; no watermark; print-quality and high legibility. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line, exact phrase in double quotes, params appended> 2) LINE BREAKS: <show how the phrase wraps> 3) PALETTE: <hex codes + roles>. Output nothing else.

Title: Step-by-Step Infographic Card: Numbered Steps That Spell Right
Teaser: Turns any process into a branded step-by-step infographic where the heading and each numbered step render legibly.
PromptHint: Give the [HEADING], a list of [STEPS] (short lines), and an optional brand [PALETTE] as hex codes.
Prompt: You are an infographic designer using Ideogram V3 to render clean, legible step cards. Build ONE numbered how-to infographic. Render the EXACT heading (in double quotes): "[HEADING]". Render these EXACT short steps as a numbered list, each verbatim: [STEPS]. Brand palette (optional hex codes): [PALETTE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): how many steps fit cleanly, the grid layout (single vertical list or 2-column), and how to keep every step line short enough to render crisply. Then write ONE Ideogram prompt specifying: a tidy infographic layout with a bold heading at the top, numbered step rows each with a small simple icon and a short label, consistent spacing, a named clean sans for body text, the supplied palette (or a tasteful default expressed as explicit hex codes), and a flat modern style. Append parameters on the same line: 'Style: Design, Aspect ratio: 4:5, Magic Prompt: Off'. RULES: spell the heading and every step exactly; keep each step to a few words so text stays sharp; correct, sequential numbers; no garbled micro-text; no watermark. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line, heading and steps in double quotes, params appended> 2) STEP COUNT: <how many steps and the layout used> 3) LEGIBILITY TIP: <one line on keeping step text crisp>. Output nothing else.

Title: Hand-Lettered Greeting Card: Occasion Message Spelled Perfectly
Teaser: Makes a warm hand-lettered card with the exact greeting and a sweet inside line, in a style that fits the occasion.
PromptHint: Set the [OCCASION], the [FRONT GREETING], an optional [INSIDE LINE], and a [STYLE] (florals, retro, kids, elegant).
Prompt: You are a greeting-card illustrator writing Ideogram V3 prompts where hand-lettering must spell correctly. Occasion: [OCCASION]. Render the EXACT front greeting (verbatim, in double quotes): "[FRONT GREETING]". Optional smaller inside-style line (verbatim): "[INSIDE LINE]". Style: [STYLE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): whether script, brush or rounded sans hand-lettering best matches the occasion and style, and how illustrative elements can frame the words without crowding them. Then write ONE Ideogram prompt specifying: a charming card front with the greeting as hand-lettered focal text, decorative illustration that borders rather than overlaps the text, exact spelling and casing, a soft cohesive palette as explicit hex codes, and a warm balanced lighting feel. Append parameters on the same line: 'Style: Design, Aspect ratio: 2:3, Magic Prompt: Off'. RULES: spell the greeting and inside line exactly with correct punctuation; keep lettering legible (script must remain readable); no gibberish flourishes that look like extra letters; no watermark. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line, exact text in double quotes, params appended> 2) PALETTE: <hex codes + roles> 3) LETTERING TIP: <one line on keeping script readable>. Output nothing else.

Title: Book / E-Book Cover Concept: Title and Author That Render Crisp
Teaser: Designs a genre-appropriate cover where the exact title, subtitle and author name spell perfectly with strong hierarchy.
PromptHint: Give [TITLE], optional [SUBTITLE], [AUTHOR NAME], and the [GENRE/MOOD] (thriller, self-help, fantasy, business).
Prompt: You are a book-cover art director using Ideogram V3 where the typography must spell correctly and signal the genre. Render these EXACT strings (verbatim, each in double quotes): title "[TITLE]", optional subtitle "[SUBTITLE]", author "[AUTHOR NAME]". Genre/mood: [GENRE/MOOD]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): the cover hierarchy (title dominant, author smaller, subtitle supporting), the type style and color language readers expect for this genre, and a central image or texture that supports rather than fights the text. Then write ONE Ideogram prompt specifying: a portrait book-cover composition with the title as the largest element, the author name placed conventionally (top or bottom), the subtitle in smaller tracked type, a named typeface feel matched to genre, a 2-3 color palette as explicit hex codes, and a tasteful background motif. Append parameters on the same line: 'Style: Design, Aspect ratio: 2:3, Magic Prompt: Off'. RULES: spell every string exactly with correct casing; no duplicate title text; no garbled author name; legible at thumbnail size; no watermark. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line, all strings in double quotes, params appended> 2) HIERARCHY: <size order of the three text blocks> 3) PALETTE: <hex codes + roles>. Output nothing else.

Title: Sale / Promo Banner: Offer Copy and CTA That Stay Readable
Teaser: Generates a high-converting promo banner with the headline, offer and call-to-action spelled exactly and laid out for scan-ability.
PromptHint: Set the [HEADLINE], the [OFFER] (e.g. 30% OFF), the [CTA] button text, and an optional brand [PALETTE].
Prompt: You are a conversion-focused marketing designer using Ideogram V3 for promo banners with crisp, on-brand text. Render these EXACT strings (verbatim, each in double quotes): headline "[HEADLINE]", offer "[OFFER]", call-to-action button "[CTA]". Brand palette (optional hex): [PALETTE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): the read order a scrolling viewer follows (headline, then the offer as the boldest element, then the CTA), and how to keep all three legible against any background. Then write ONE Ideogram prompt specifying: a clean promotional banner layout, the offer as the largest punchy element, a clearly styled CTA that looks like a tappable button, a named bold sans, the supplied palette (or a high-contrast default expressed as explicit hex codes), and a simple supporting graphic or product silhouette that never covers the text. Append parameters on the same line: 'Style: Design, Aspect ratio: 16:9, Magic Prompt: Off' (swap to '1:1' for a square feed banner). RULES: spell every string exactly including the percent or number in the offer; keep copy short; no gibberish fine print; high contrast for legibility; no watermark. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line, all strings in double quotes, params appended> 2) READ ORDER: <how the eye should travel> 3) PALETTE: <hex codes + roles>. Output nothing else.

Title: Caption My Photo: Add a Perfect, Legible Overlay to an Uploaded Image
Teaser: Conversational image-edit prompt that keeps your photo and face untouched, then overlays an exact caption that renders cleanly.
PromptHint: Upload a photo, set the [CAPTION] text, the [PLACEMENT] (top, bottom, corner), and a [TEXT STYLE].
Prompt: You are an editor who writes natural-language image-edit prompts for text-capable models (Ideogram remix/edit) that render legible text on an EXISTING uploaded photo. Goal: keep the uploaded image, subject and face exactly as-is, and overlay a caption that spells correctly. Render this EXACT caption (verbatim, in double quotes): "[CAPTION]". Placement: [PLACEMENT]. Text style: [TEXT STYLE] (e.g. bold sans lower-third, handwritten marker, vintage serif title, neon sign). THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): where the caption sits without covering the subject's face, and whether it needs a subtle band, shadow or outline to stay readable over the photo. Then write ONE conversational prompt that: explicitly says to preserve the same photo, composition, face and lighting; describes the caption text, its exact placement and the named text style; and specifies a contrast treatment (drop shadow, semi-transparent bar, or stroke) so the words stay crisp. End the line with 'keep everything else unchanged, Magic Prompt: Off' so the edit stays literal and the caption is preserved. RULES: render the caption EXACTLY with correct spelling, casing and punctuation; do not alter or beautify the subject's face; no extra text; no watermark. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste conversational line, caption in double quotes, instruction appended> 2) CONTRAST TIP: <one line on keeping text readable over the photo> 3) FALLBACK: <one alternate placement if the caption clashes with the subject>. Output nothing else.

Title: Single-Word Art Print: Meaning Hidden Inside the Letterforms
Teaser: Creates a gallery-style single-word art piece where the illustration lives inside the letters and the word spells perfectly.
PromptHint: Put one word in [WORD], the [CONCEPT] to weave into the letters, and an [ART STYLE] (line art, watercolor, 3D, retro).
Prompt: You are a typographic artist using Ideogram V3 to make a single-word art print where imagery is built into the letters. Render this EXACT word as the entire composition (verbatim, in double quotes): "[WORD]". Concept to express through the letterforms: [CONCEPT]. Art style: [ART STYLE]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): how the chosen concept can fill or decorate each letter (negative space, scenes inside the strokes, textures) while keeping the word instantly readable. Then write ONE Ideogram prompt specifying: large display letterforms spelling the word, the [CONCEPT] integrated INTO the letters (not separate clip-art beside them), the named art style and rendering, a cohesive palette as explicit hex codes, a clean background that lets the word pop, and generous margins for a framed print. Append parameters on the same line: 'Style: Design, Aspect ratio: 1:1, Magic Prompt: Off' (swap to '2:3' for a portrait print). RULES: the word must spell exactly and stay clearly legible despite the decoration; no extra letters or duplicate words; one word only; no watermark. OUTPUT FORMAT exactly: 1) PROMPT: <one copy-paste line, the word in double quotes, params appended> 2) LETTER IDEA: <one line on how the concept fills the letters> 3) PALETTE: <hex codes + roles>. Output nothing else.
